title: carlina graeca var. rothii
intro: Carlina graeca var. rothii is a variety of the Carlina graeca plant, also known as the carline thistle or Roth's carline thistle, a flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Carlina graeca var. rothii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
